items.name: Africa
facts: Africa is the second-largest continent.
The Sahara Desert stretches across part of northern Africa.
Algeria is the largest country in Africa by area.
Lagos is a major city name students can connect with Africa.
funFacts: Africa appears on world maps as one of the seven large land areas on Earth.
54 is the approximate number of countries students learn for Africa.
Algeria and Lagos are both useful names to remember in Africa.
clues: This place is one of the seven continents.
It is mostly in the Mostly Northern and Southern hemisphere.
It has about 54 countries.
Its largest country is Algeria.
A major city linked with it is Lagos.
Geography lessons group many countries inside this continent.
On a globe, this answer is a major land area.
Students often connect it with Algeria.
The city Lagos helps identify this continent.
It is taught as a continent mostly in the Mostly Northern and Southern.
World maps show this continent with about 54 countries.
Algeria is a strong clue for this continent.
If the hemisphere clue is Mostly Northern and Southern, this could be the answer.
The city clue Lagos points toward this continent.
This answer is not a country or state; it is a continent.
